title: buildah bud gets unmarshal error when building
body:
Dockerfile is here: https://github.com/Skatteetaten/wingnut/blob/master/src/main/docker/Dockerfile

The following works: `docker build --build-arg javaMinorVersion=144 --build-arg javaBuildVersion=01-r0 --build-arg jolokiaVersion=1.3.7 --build-arg baseImageVersion=buildah -t foo/bar . `

The following does not work
`buildah bud --build-arg javaMinorVersion=144 --build-arg javaBuildVersion=01-r0 --build-arg jolokiaVersion=1.3.7 --build-arg baseImageVersion=buildah -t wingnutBuildah . `

**Buildah version:**
Version:       0.4
Go Version:    go1.8
Image Spec:    1.0.0
Runtime Spec:  1.0.0
Git Commit:    5cc3c51
Built:         Fri Oct 20 07:48:26 2017
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64

**Docker version:**
Client:
 Version:      1.12.6
 API version:  1.24
 Go version:   go1.6.2
 Git commit:   78d1802
 Built:        Tue Jan 31 23:35:14 2017
 OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

Server:
 Version:      1.12.6
 API version:  1.24
 Go version:   go1.6.2
 Git commit:   78d1802
 Built:        Tue Jan 31 23:35:14 2017
 OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```
**ErrorMessage**

`STEP 1: FROM alpine:3.6
STEP 2: ARG javaMajorVersion=8
STEP 3: ARG javaMinorVersion
STEP 4: ARG javaBuildVersion
STEP 5: ARG jolokiaVersion
STEP 6: ARG baseImageVersion
STEP 7: MAINTAINER The Norwegian Tax Administration <utvpaas@skatteetaten.no>
STEP 8: LABEL java=openjdk-${JAVA_VERSION_MAJOR}u${JAVA_VERSION_MINOR}-b${JAVA_VERSION_BUILD}       alpine=3.6       jolokia=${JOLOKIA_VERSION}       version=${BASE_IMAGE_VERSION}
STEP 9: ENV JAVA_HOME /usr/lib/jvm/java-1.${javaMajorVersion}-openjdk
STEP 10: ENV JAVA_VERSION_MAJOR=${javaMajorVersion}     JAVA_VERSION_MINOR=${javaMinorVersion}     JAVA_VERSION_BUILD=${javaBuildVersion}     JAVA_PACKAGE=openjdk${javaMajorVersion}-jre     PATH=${PATH}:$JAVA_HOME/jre/bin     LANG=C.UTF-8     HOME=/u01     JOLOKIA_VERSION=${jolokiaVersion}     JOLOKIA_PATH=/opt/jolokia/jolokia-jvm-${jolokiaVersion}-agent.jar     BASE_IMAGE_VERSION=${baseImageVersion}     ALPINE_VERSION=3.6    TRUST_STORE=$JAVA_HOME/jre/lib/security/cacerts
STEP 11: ADD jolokia /opt/jolokia
STEP 12: RUN chmod -R 777 /opt/jolokia &&     apk add "openjdk8-jre=${JAVA_VERSION_MAJOR}.${JAVA_VERSION_MINOR}.${JAVA_VERSION_BUILD}" -f --update-cache --repository http://nl.alpinelinux.org/alpine/edge/community &&     apk add --no-cache       dumb-init       drill       tcpdump       ngrep       bash       tzdata &&     mkdir $HOME
json: cannot unmarshal object into Go value of type []string
error building: error building at step {Env:[TRUST_STORE=/usr/lib/jvm/java-1.8-openjdk/jre/lib/security/cacerts ALPINE_VERSION=3.6 BASE_IMAGE_VERSION=buildah JOLOKIA_PATH=/opt/jolokia/jolokia-jvm-1.3.7-agent.jar JOLOKIA_VERSION=1.3.7 HOME=/u01 LANG=C.UTF-8 PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin:/usr/lib/jvm/java-1.8-openjdk/jre/bin JAVA_PACKAGE=openjdk8-jre JAVA_VERSION_BUILD=01-r0 JAVA_VERSION_MINOR=144 JAVA_VERSION_MAJOR=8 JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-1.8-openjdk PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin              JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-1.8-openjdk JAVA_VERSION_MAJOR=8 JAVA_VERSION_MINOR=144 JAVA_VERSION_BUILD=01-r0 JAVA_PACKAGE=openjdk8-jre PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin:/usr/lib/jvm/java-1.8-openjdk/jre/bin LANG=C.UTF-8 HOME=/u01 JOLOKIA_VERSION=1.3.7 JOLOKIA_PATH=/opt/jolokia/jolokia-jvm-1.3.7-agent.jar BASE_IMAGE_VERSION=buildah ALPINE_VERSION=3.6 TRUST_STORE=/usr/lib/jvm/java-1.8-openjdk/jre/lib/security/cacerts javaMinorVersion=144 javaBuildVersion=01-r0 jolokiaVersion=1.3.7 baseImageVersion=buildah javaMajorVersion=8] Command:run Args:[chmod -R 777 /opt/jolokia &&     apk add "openjdk8-jre=${JAVA_VERSION_MAJOR}.${JAVA_VERSION_MINOR}.${JAVA_VERSION_BUILD}" -f --update-cache --repository http://nl.alpinelinux.org/alpine/edge/community &&     apk add --no-cache       dumb-init       drill       tcpdump       ngrep       bash       tzdata &&     mkdir $HOME] Flags:[] Attrs:map[] Message:RUN chmod -R 777 /opt/jolokia &&     apk add "openjdk8-jre=${JAVA_VERSION_MAJOR}.${JAVA_VERSION_MINOR}.${JAVA_VERSION_BUILD}" -f --update-cache --repository http://nl.alpinelinux.org/alpine/edge/community &&     apk add --no-cache       dumb-init       drill       tcpdump       ngrep       bash       tzdata &&     mkdir $HOME Original:RUN chmod -R 777 /opt/jolokia &&     apk add "openjdk8-jre=${JAVA_VERSION_MAJOR}.${JAVA_VERSION_MINOR}.${JAVA_VERSION_BUILD}" -f --update-cache --repository http://nl.alpinelinux.org/alpine/edge/community &&     apk add --no-cache       dumb-init       drill       tcpdump       ngrep       bash       tzdata &&     mkdir $HOME}: exit status 1
`
```

comments.body:
What OS are you running on?

rpm -q buildah

Ubuntu 16.04

I followed the build instructuons on the wiki.

Sent from my iPhone

> On 20 Oct 2017, at 13:40, Daniel J Walsh <notifications@github.com> wrote:
> 
> What OS are you running on?
> 
> rpm -q buildah
> 
> —
> You are receiving this because you authored the thread.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub, or mute the thread.
>

Thanks for the report, if you've the time, it would be great if you could try this and report the output.
```
buildah --debug bud --build-arg javaMinorVersion=144 --build-arg javaBuildVersion=01-r0 --build-arg jolokiaVersion=1.3.7 --build-arg baseImageVersion=buildah -t wingnutBuildah .
```
@bjartek TYVM for running that and posting.  The one thing that pops is the issue with runc.  Can you tell me what version of runc you're running please?

runc version spec: 1.0.0-rc2-dev
it is the one i got when running the make install command of buildah like posted in the wiki

I ran this
` make runc all TAGS="apparmor seccomp"`

The only thing i did differently from the guide was use a prebuild go version since i had it set up and i put buildah inside go/src/github.com
I see that rc4 of runc is out. Should I try IT?

That copy of `runc` that we build is there for the sake of the integration tests, so that we know it's a version that's compatible with the configuration we produce for it when handling RUN instructions, but it's not something we currently install when you run `make install`.  Based on what we saw in #291, I'd definitely recommend updating the `runc` package and giving it another go.
Yes please upgrade runc as @nalind suggested.  I was just reviewing the install instructions and trying to upgrade on my vm.

I can confirm that it works with RC-4 of runc. I recommend updating the docs on how to install on ubuntu :) 

Thanks for excellent help!

@bjartek 🥇 

Thanks for the help and pointers.  I'll work on updating the documentation, I think we might actually have the same issue in Fedora.
#296 to address this for at least the short term.  Still looking at how to best update the install instructions.

So, for buildah on ubuntu, I'll need to make sure that we're using our fork of runc and not the distro's runc which is usually older than what we ship in PPA.

@nalind @TomSweeneyRedHat could you let me know the easiest way to setup buildah to use a custom runc binary?

@lsm5 First, fwiw, I just installed a fresh Fedora and then did 'dnf -y install buildah'.  The rc4 variant of runc was installed there.  So I think Fedora is just fine.

As far as Ubuntu goes, what we're recommending is documented on our README.md  (https://github.com/projectatomic/buildah).  That doc says:

```
  apt-get -y install software-properties-common
  add-apt-repository -y ppa:alexlarsson/flatpak
  add-apt-repository -y ppa:gophers/archive
  apt-add-repository -y ppa:projectatomic/ppa
  apt-get -y -qq update
  apt-get -y install bats btrfs-tools git libapparmor-dev libdevmapper-dev libglib2.0-dev libgpgme11-dev libostree-dev libseccomp-dev libselinux1-dev skopeo-containers go-md2man
  apt-get -y install golang-1.8

```

Should we add a apt-get for runc 1 rc4 here?

sure, but also, the fork of runc we ship (`cri-o-runc`) will install runc at `/usr/lib/cri-o-runc/bin/runc` so as to not conflict with `/usr/bin/runc` used by the distro package. We gotta make sure that buildah will use the former and not the latter. What's the best way to do that?

I don't think Buildah has a way currently to toggle the location of the runc.  My only thought would be to add the new locale to the front of the PATH env variable, but I'm not sure if that's wise.  I just installed buildah directly using 'apt-get -y install buildah' and then also by pulling a clone from github and compiling by hand (also as documented in the README.md) and both left runc alone.

@nalind, thoughts?

Manipulating `$PATH` so that a specific `runc` gets called, or using the `--runtime` flag when using the `bud` and `run` commands to specify the pathname for the command, are currently the only ways we have for changing how we run processes in containers.
@nalind, I'd completely forgotten the --runtime command, good thought.  @lsm5, are you concerned about our buildah ubuntu kit just replacing/upgrading runc?

On Mon, Oct 23, 2017 at 09:20:35AM -0700, Tom Sweeney wrote:
> @nalind, I'd completely forgotten the --runtime command, good thought.  @lsm5, are you concerned about our buildah ubuntu kit just replacing/upgrading runc?

So, buildah ubuntu package won't mess with distro's runc at all because I
will enforce a dependency on `cri-o-runc` which is totally isolated from
distro's runc. I only need to make sure the buildah ends up
`/usr/lib/cri-o-runc/bin/runc` and not /usr/bin/runc, which I guess gets
addressed by @nalind's comment above.

And that looks like something handled by the user and not the packager. Correct me
if I'm wrong.

-- 
Lokesh
IRC: lsm5
GPG: 0xC7C3A0DD
https://keybase.io/lsm5

On Mon, Oct 23, 2017 at 01:56:55PM -0400, Lokesh Mandvekar wrote:
> On Mon, Oct 23, 2017 at 09:20:35AM -0700, Tom Sweeney wrote:
> > @nalind, I'd completely forgotten the --runtime command, good thought.  @lsm5, are you concerned about our buildah ubuntu kit just replacing/upgrading runc?
> 
> So, buildah ubuntu package won't mess with distro's runc at all because I
> will enforce a dependency on `cri-o-runc` which is totally isolated from
> distro's runc. I only need to make sure the buildah ends up
> `/usr/lib/cri-o-runc/bin/runc` and not /usr/bin/runc, which I guess gets
> addressed by @nalind's comment above.
> 
> And that looks like something handled by the user and not the packager. Correct me
> if I'm wrong.
> 

Of course, I gotta make sure that `apt install buildah` does pull in
`cri-o-runc` as well, for which I will do a new deb update.

HTH.
-- 
Lokesh
IRC: lsm5
GPG: 0xC7C3A0DD
https://keybase.io/lsm5
I built buildah 0.4 an hour back which depends on cri-o-runc. Let me know how that works.
